title: Wave 5b — Conditional gateway
goal: Expose conditional SOL buy order creation through the Compass MCP gateway with policy, approval metadata, and audit, without implementing keeper execution or signing.

reviewBudget.warning: Combined Wave 5 was requested, but conditional work must remain a separate reviewable track. Pause and split before implementation if swap work already consumes the budget.

tasks.title: Add RED conditional gateway/policy tests
tasks.description: Specify valid order approval, missing oracle/target/expiry context, expired/invalid order fail-closed, default approval, metadata fingerprints, and no legacy imports.

evidence.expected: RED before conditional gateway/policy branch exists
evidence.actual: RED as expected: conditional gateway tests failed because conditionalGateway/Contracts did not exist; policyEngine conditional tests failed because conditional_buy still fell through to policy.default (9 failed / 35 passed across focused files).

tasks.title: Implement conditional contracts and policy surface
tasks.description: Add conditional contracts and the smallest conservative policy branch/defaults needed for order-creation decisions. Keep schemas/types separate from behavior.

evidence.actual: GREEN: conditional policy/default schema and gateway contracts/behavior passed focused tests for default approval, missing context, expired orders, unsafe oracle evidence, and policy loader updates (51 tests / 3 files).

tasks.title: Implement conditional gateway evaluation
tasks.description: Create evaluateConditionalGateway with action candidate, policy context, fail-closed gate, approval metadata, and redacted audit-safe data.

evidence.actual: GREEN: conditional gateway evaluated action candidates, policy context, proposal gating, approval metadata fingerprints, fail-closed reasons, and no-legacy import coverage (51 tests / 3 files with policy/loadPolicy).

tasks.title: Add RED MCP conditional tests
tasks.description: Specify tools/list and tools/call behavior for create_conditional_buy_sol and optional oracle simulation, with audit and direct-signing regression.

evidence.expected: RED until conditional tools are registered/routed
evidence.actual: RED as expected: registry/server list tests failed because conditional tools were absent; router tests failed because simulate/create conditional calls were unknown/unrouted and did not call evaluateConditionalGateway (5 failed / 15 passed).

tasks.title: Register and route conditional tools
tasks.description: Extend MCP contracts, registry, router, and result metadata typing for conditional order creation while keeping signing blocked.

evidence.actual: GREEN: MCP registry/router/server tests passed with simulate_conditional_buy_oracle_check and create_conditional_buy_sol listed/routed, oracle simulation ALLOW, conditional creation REQUIRE_HUMAN_APPROVAL, invalid conditional input REQUIRE_ADDITIONAL_CONTEXT, and direct signing regressions intact (20 tests / 3 files).

tasks.title: Validate and pause for review
tasks.description: Run full validation and stop for parent review. Do not implement keeper execution or signer behavior in this wave.

evidence.actual: PASS: full backend Vitest suite passed after final conditional gateway adjustment (120 tests / 12 files).
PASS with existing app/layout.tsx react-refresh/only-export-components warning; 0 errors.
PASS: TypeScript no-emit completed with no errors.

acceptanceCriteria: Conditional order creation is a first-party MCP tool and never a legacy chat flow.
Valid creation remains policy-bound and auditable.
Missing oracle, target, amount, expiry, or slippage evidence fails closed.
Keeper execution and signing are not implemented.
